data.title: Incannex Healthcare prices $12.5M private placement
data.description:
• Incannex Healthcare (NASDAQ:IXHL) said on Friday that it has entered into security purchase agreements for the purchase and sale of 11.57M shares of the company’s common stock for a purchase price of $1.08 per share and Series A common stock warrants at an initial exercise price of $2.16 per share.
  • The offering is being priced at the market under Nasdaq rules.
  • Aggregate gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be approximately $12.5M.
  • Incannex intends to use the net proceeds from the private placement to fund ongoing clinical trials, including the completion and topline readout of the U.S. Phase 2 study of IHL-42X, and its expansion into the Phase 3 portion of the study, with sites in the U.K. and the U.S.
  • The proceeds will also be used for the repayment of outstanding convertible debentures, as working capital, and for general corporate purposes.
  • Incannex Healthcare (IXHL) shares were down more than 6% during Friday trading hours.

data.title: Bitcoin on course to see weekly rise as Trump establishes Strategic Bitcoin Reserve
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is set to rise 4.6% on a weekly basis as President Donald Trump established a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve and a United States Digital Asset Stockpile through an executive order.

The Bitcoin in the reserve is not to be sold. BTC fell sharply after the announcement, and Ethereum (ETH-USD), XRP (XRP-USD), Solana (SOL-USD), and Cardano (ADA-USD) were also down.

The world’s largest digital currency has seen an eventful week, as it touched $94.4K on Monday but dropped as low as $81.5K in the same week.

The volatile moves came as Trump announced plans to create a national crypto reserve on Sunday. The crypto market, including Bitcoin (BTC-USD) initially hit a euphoric state on the announcement, but it didn't take long for the rally to fade as a slew of industry insiders scrutinized the updated reserve plan and the intentions behind it.

“Given Bitcoin’s drop last week, it’s highly likely that institutions have decided to move away from risk-on assets; the crypto reserve announcement is unlikely to change this as geopolitical tensions and tariff issues are much greater risk factors looming above investors’ heads,” Jeff Mei, COO at BTSE, said.

We see Bitcoin even sinking back to around the low $80K range, and it may stay there until the Federal Reserve decides to resume rate cuts later this year, Mei added.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) could not hold on to the gains and on Monday fell down to $86.1K. Earlier, Trump had announced plans to build a strategic crypto reserve with BTC, Ethereum (ETH-USD), XRP (XRP-USD), Solana (SOL-USD), and Cardano (ADA-USD).

“Persistent volatility in the crypto market ahead of the Trump-hosted crypto summit, potentially impacting Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies significantly,” SA contributor Investing Experts Podcast said.

Bitcoin is increasingly viewed as "digital gold," with adoption by corporations, countries, and wealthy investors as a hedge against dollar strength, Investing Experts Podcast added.

BTC, Ether (ETH-USD) and other token prices reversed higher in Wednesday morning trading, as persistent volatility continued to grip the crypto market ahead of Trump's much-anticipated crypto summit.

The crypto summit will be held at the White House on Friday.

“We expect more announcements from Trump's crypto summit that can further boost the industry, as he promises to make the U.S. the 'crypto capital of the world.' While last week's ETF flows were highly negative, with more than a $1 billion in outflows from BTC and ETH ETFs, we anticipate investors will reverse course as the crypto industry shows signs of a bottom and continuation of the bull market,” Chris Yu, Co-Founder and CEO of SignalPlus said.

For the rest of the week, the price of BTC remained under pressure, but it did briefly go over $90K on Wednesday, Thursday and Friday.

However, J.P. Morgan cited that the cryptocurrencies may likely remain under pressure in the near term in a recent research note published on Wednesday.

"February's close to 20% decline in the bitcoin price was accompanied by heavy spot bitcoin ETFs selling, suggesting that retail investors have played a significant role in the recent correction," the analyst wrote. "February's $3.5bn spot bitcoin ETF outflow was the highest monthly outflow since their launch."

More Notable News

Market-making powerhouse Jump is restoring its crypto operations in the U.S. after dialing back in recent years amid heightened regulatory scrutiny, according to a media report dated Wednesday.

Bakkt (BKKT) soared 18% after a report that Apex Fintech is evaluating a potential takeover bid for the crypto trading company.

Coinbase Global (COIN) CEO Brian Armstrong heralded a "golden age" for crypto, celebrating a landmark victory over the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission that he said clears the path for innovation after years of regulatory battles.

In its latest in a string of dropping suits against crypto companies, the U.S. Securities and Exchange staff agreed in principle to dismiss its lawsuit against Kraken, the crypto trading platform said in a statement

Riot Platforms (RIOT) produced 470 bitcoins in February. MARA (MARA) produced 706 bitcoins in February, down 6% sequentially. CleanSpark (CLSK) produced 624 bitcoins in the month.

White House artificial intelligence and cryptocurrency czar David Sacks confirmed that he has sold all of his direct crypto holdings investments before the Trump administration's official start on Jan. 20, according to a recent post on social media platform X.

Strategy (MSTR) said it did not buy any Bitcoin (BTC-USD) in the period between February 24 and March 2.

Crypto moves

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) down 0.04% to $88.9K as of 2:14 pm on Friday. Ether (ETH-USD) was down 0.5% to $2.2K, while DOGE (DOGE-USD) up 2.8%.

 

 

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: U.S. drilling rigs tick lower after five straight weekly gains in Baker Hughes survey
data.description:
The number of active rigs drilling for oil and gas in the U.S. fell by 1 to 592, following five straight weekly gains, Baker Hughes said Friday in its latest report.

Drilling rigs targeting crude oil in the U.S. stayed flat at 486 while gas rigs dropped by 1 to 101 and 5 rigs remained classified as miscellaneous.

Rigs targeting oil in the Permian Basin lost 1 to 301, the oil count in the Eagle Ford remained unchanged at 43, and the Williston Basin count also stayed flat at 32.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Dividend ‘hopefuls’ – stocks have the ability to pay dividends but don't
data.description:
Morgan Stanley analysts presented a list of stocks that have the ability to initiate a regular dividend, due to their net cash and free cash flow yields – calling them “dividend hopefuls.”

The following list has the goal of “generating new ideas and capturing additional alpha,” said Todd Castagno, head of Global Valuation, Accounting & Tax within Morgan Stanley's Research division.

These companies “should have the ability” to pay a dividend and potentially provide larger returns. The list names companies that don't currently pay a quarterly dividend, but have a net cash position that is greater than 5% of market cap, and generate a free cash flow yield of more than 3%.

“In periods of slow growth, dividends become more important and make up a larger percentage of investors' total returns,” said Castagno. “With low growth comes a declining interest rate environment, which makes durable, higher yielding dividends relatively more attractive as cash and fixed income become less lucrative.”

Here are the “dividend initiator hopefuls”

  • Lyft, Inc. (LYFT) – Net cash % of market cap: 13.7%; FCF Yield: 15.0%
  • Brighthouse Financial, Inc. (BHF) – Net cash % of market cap: 56.0%; FCF Yield: 14.3%
  • TripAdvisor, Inc. (TRIP) – Net cash % of market cap: 8.0%; FCF Yield: 13.8%

data.title: Oracle Q3 Preview: Analysts see stable cloud infra growth, Stargate remains in focus
data.description:
Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) is expected to post an over 8% rise in its third-quarter revenue on Monday, March 10 with analysts seeing stable growth in cloud infrastructure with the Q3 print dominated by comments around the Stargate Project.

In the previous quarter, the company had reported figures below the analysts’ estimates across most key metrics including EPS, total revenue and cloud revenue.

The Stargate Project, announced at the beginning of the year, would involve close collaboration between Oracle, NVIDIA (NVDA), and OpenAI to build and operate the computing system. UBS analysts expect some insight this quarter regarding the potential financial impact of the project, which it says could be a positive catalyst.

The United States’ $500B AI initiative aims to build data centers across the country in four years, starting in Texas.

“Two separate checks (both part of the Stargate data center team) told us that the first OpenAI Abilene GPU cluster (estimated at ~130,000 GPUs) will go live in May. This implies a material potential lift to Oracle’s OCI segment beginning in the 1Q/Aug quarter, as well as the potential for a large OpenAI commitment impacting the backlog as early as the 3Q/Feb quarter,” UBS said.

Both UBS and Jefferies have maintained their Buy rating on the stock. Seeking Alpha and Wall Street analysts are also bullish on the ORCL and rated the stock a Buy. However, Seeking Alpha’s quant ratings recommend to Hold Oracle, largely dragged down by its growth and valuation factor.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen no upward revisions, compared to 27 downward revisions, while revenue estimates have seen one upward revision versus 23 downward moves.

In the past one year, ORCL has gained nearly 32%, outperforming the nearly 11% rise in the S&P500 Index. On a year-to-date basis, however, it has lost a little over 9%.

data.title: U.S. stocks churn after jobs report, drive toward weekly loss
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages seesawed Friday afternoon, but remained on course for losses for the week, during which investors wrestled with multiple tariff announcements from the Trump administration. 

The S&P 500 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) were each up 0.2% after losing more than 1% earlier. The Dow (DJI) +0.2%. The Nasdaq-100 (NDX) turned up +0.3% after dipping into correction territory. 

U.S. President Donald Trump during the session said reciprocal tariffs on Canadian dairy and lumber products may be announced as early as Friday. This week, he halted tariffs imposed on certain goods from Canada and Mexico until early April. 

Stocks fell sharply earlier Friday after February U.S. nonfarm payrolls rose less than expected. In bonds, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.24%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 4 basis points to 3.92%.

Equities were set for big losses for the week that rounded off with the February nonfarm payrolls. The S&P 500 (SP500) was looking at ~3% weekly decline. 

"The week confirmed that tariffs remain a wildcard for market confidence, and traders are exhausted from the back-and-forth," Mark Malek, chief investment officer at Siebert Financial, said in a note. Markets have moved "painfully in the red" even after U.S. President Donald Trump this week delayed some tariffs on goods from Canada and Mexico for a month, Malek said. 

The February jobs report showed additions of +151K, below the 160K consensus. The unemployment rate edged up to 4.1% and average weekly wage growth increased 0.3%, cooling from January. 

The "markets should breathe a sigh of relief that there wasn’t a shock in either direction and the report was a mixed bag. The takeaway is that wage data declined which is disinflationary," Gina Bolvin, president of Bolvin Wealth Management Group, said. 

Traders on Friday raised the odds of a June interest rate cut by the Fed and pared the probability of a May reduction. 

In equities, HP Enterprise (HPE) -15% following a lower-than expected outlook stemming from tariffs and competitive pricing. 

Broadcom (AVGO) +7% after the semiconductor and software giant reported first-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations.

data.title: Funko's soft guidance rattles investors
data.description:
Funko (NASDAQ:FNKO) is in the red for the ninth time in ten days with a new 7-month low after the company’s outlook for sluggish sales of its novelty collectibles in Q1 and FY25 spooked investors and drove shares down another 8% on Friday.

As the company focuses on the execution of its strategic growth plan which revolves around financial discipline and diversification, Funko (NASDAQ:FNKO) expects “measured” top-line growth in 2025, weighted towards the second half of the year.

This translates into first quarter sales of $188M to $198M and an adjusted net loss of $0.48 to $0.40 per share which compares to Q1 2024 results of $215.7M in sales and a loss of $0.17 per share, as well as a deceleration from Q4.

For the full year, Funko (NASDAQ:FNKO) is forecasting sales to be between $1.05B to $1.082B, unchanged to slightly lower than in 2024 and below the consensus estimate of $1.09B.

With approximately a third of Funko’s (FNKO) global product purchases coming from China, this guidance assumes 20% tariffs on imports from China, as well as weakening consumer behavior in the U.S. market.

“Similar to others in our industry, we’re experiencing softening consumer behavior in the U.S. market,” Funko CEO Cynthia Williams acknowledged on the company’s earnings call with analysts, singling out tariffs and inflation for heightened concern.

For the reported quarter, a 10.6% gain in the sale of its Pop! collectibles helped offset double-digit losses for Loungefly and all other collectibles, resulting in a 0.9% increase in total sales to $293.7M, $7.6M better than expected. The company also earned a profit of $0.08 per share, which beat expectations to break even.
